title: IBM Cloudant service passwords should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don’t need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: IBM Cloudant connection URL with embedded password detected. This URL contains authentication credentials in the format 'https://username:password@account.cloudant.com' which provides full access to the Cloudant database service. Exposing this connection string allows unauthorized parties to read, write, and delete database documents, access sensitive data, and potentially compromise the entire database. Remove credentials from URLs and use IAM authentication or store them securely in a secrets manager.

IBM Cloudant service password detected. Cloudant legacy credentials use randomly generated 24-64 character alphanumeric passwords that provide authenticated access to NoSQL database operations. When paired with a username, this password grants full CRUD (Create, Read, Update, Delete) access to all databases and documents in the Cloudant instance. Exposing this password could allow unauthorized data access, data manipulation, data deletion, and potential data breaches. Migrate to IAM authentication and revoke legacy credentials immediately.
